P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUiLDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance/ Admini§tr otiOI1 
Executive/legal 
••• 
Enforcement 
Conflict of Inter',,' 
(916) 3225660 
Assemblyman Gordon Duffy 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Dear Assemblyman Duffy: 
322-5901 
322-6441 
January 25, 1978 
Thank you for your letter of January 17, 1978, in 
which you asked whether you had to report on your State-
ment of Economic Interests a lifetime membership 
received several years ago. 
According to Bill Kitchen 
of the California Optometric Association, the donor of 
the membership, it appears that the primary benefit of 
your membership is the receipt of a monthly newsletter 
and the privilege of joining their credit union and life 
insurance program. 
He indicated to me that the cost of 
an active membership could be as much as $304 per year, 
which pays for the cost of running the Association. 
The Political Reform Act requires each public 
official to report gifts of $25 or more received during 
the reporting period. 
Government Code Sections 87203 and 
87207. 
However, the Political Reform Act does not require 
reporting the use during a current filing period of gifts 
received in prior periods. 
From the facts provided to us, 
it appears that you received your "gift" several years ago 
and are continuing to receive certain benefits from it. 
Because this gift was provided to you several years ago, 
you have no reporting obligations. 
Please feel free to calIon me if you have any 
further questions on this matter. 
:nc 
cc: 
Bill Kitchen 
Sincerely, 
M. Stern 
1 Couns 1 
322-6444 


DISTRict' 
32'f 
DOUTY, ROOM 8 
HANFORD. CALIFORNIA 93230 
582·4431

alifnruia lIl~Bislatur~ 
GORDON DUFFY 
MEMBER 01" ASSEMBL.Y 
STATE CAPITOL 
THIRTY.SECOND ASSEMBLY DISTRICT 
Daniel Lowenstein 
Chairman 
January 17, 1978 
Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Dan: 
HEAL TH 
Due to a new billing system instituted by the Cali-
fornia Optometric Association, I have been reminded of 
the fact they are reporting my lifetime membership in 
their Association which was a gift to me many years ago 
(before the Fair Political Practices Act). 
I have not practiced optometry in over 12 years and 
the only benefit I have received from my membership in 
the COA is the privilege of using their credit union. 
My questions are: 
1. 
Should I report this membership even though it 
was given to me prior to enactment of the FPCC? 
2. 
Would I need to report if only using their 
credit union? 
Thank you for your assistance. 
S 
erely,

sections.question: 1. Should I report this membership even though it was given to me prior to enactment of the FPCC? 2. Would I need to report if only using their credit union?
sections.conclusion: Because this gift was provided to you several years ago, you have no reporting obligations.
sections.question_synthetic: Assemblyman Duffy asked whether a lifetime membership in the California Optometric Association, received before the Political Reform Act was enacted, must be reported on his Statement of Economic Interests, and whether the ongoing use of the association's credit union triggers reporting obligations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that because the lifetime membership gift was received several years before the current reporting period, it does not require reporting on the Statement of Economic Interests, as the Political Reform Act does not require reporting the use of gifts received in prior periods.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (first page, Duffy's letter dated January 17, 1978) and FPPC response (second page, M. Stern's letter dated January 25, 1978). Extracted information from the FPPC response letter. Question and conclusion sections are clearly delineated in the response.

QUESTION: 1. Should I report this membership even though it was given to me prior to enactment of the FPCC? 2. Would I need to report if only using their credit union?

CONCLUSION: Because this gift was provided to you several years ago, you have no reporting obligations.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Assemblyman Gordon Duffy's inquiry about whether a lifetime membership in the California Optometric Association, received years ago before the Political Reform Act, must be reported. The FPPC determined that no reporting is required because the gift predates the current filing period.
